questions.text: How does Surah Luqman describe the Quran in its opening verses?
questions.answer: As verses of the Wise Book, serving as guidance and mercy for the doers of good (Muhsinin).

questions.text: What are the three main characteristics of the 'Muhsinin' mentioned in the beginning of the Surah?
questions.answer: They establish prayer, give Zakat, and have firm certainty in the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is 'Lahwal-Hadith' (idle talk) and what is its consequence?
questions.answer: It refers to distracting speech or entertainment used to mislead people from Allah's path; those who use it will face a humiliating punishment.

questions.text: How does a person who rejects the signs of Allah react when they are recited to him?
questions.answer: He turns away in arrogance as if he did not hear them, as if there were deafness in his ears.

questions.text: What reward is promised to those who believe and do righteous deeds?
questions.answer: The Gardens of Pleasure, where they will abide forever according to the true promise of Allah.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the creation of the heavens in this Surah?
questions.answer: He created the heavens without any pillars that you can see.

questions.text: What is the function of the mountains on earth according to this Surah?
questions.answer: They are placed as firm stabilizers so the earth does not shake with its inhabitants.

questions.text: What does the diversity of plants and animals on earth signify?
questions.answer: They are part of Allah's creation; He challenges others to show what their 'partners' have created, proving they have created nothing.

questions.text: Who was Luqman and what was he given by Allah?
questions.answer: He was a wise man who was given wisdom (Hikmah) and commanded to be grateful to Allah.

questions.text: What is the first and most important piece of advice Luqman gave to his son?
questions.answer: 'O my son, do not associate [anything] with Allah. Indeed, association [with Him] is a great injustice (Shirk).'

questions.text: What does Allah command mankind regarding their parents?
questions.answer: To be good and grateful to them, acknowledging the mother's hardship in carrying and weaning the child.

questions.text: How long is the period of weaning mentioned in the context of parental rights?
questions.answer: Two years.

questions.text: Is there an exception to the command of obeying parents?
questions.answer: Yes, if they strive to make you associate partners with Allah of which you have no knowledge, you must not obey them in that.

questions.text: How should a child treat disbelieving parents who pressure them toward Shirk?
questions.answer: One should not obey them in Shirk, but must still accompany them in this world with kindness.

questions.text: Whose path should a believer follow according to the advice on parents?
questions.answer: The path of those who turn back to Allah in repentance.

questions.text: What was Luqman's lesson about the 'mustard seed'?
questions.answer: That even a deed as small as a mustard seed, hidden in a rock or anywhere in the universe, will be brought forth by Allah for judgment.

questions.text: What four religious and social duties did Luqman command his son to perform?
questions.answer: Establish prayer, enjoin what is right, forbid what is wrong, and be patient over whatever befalls you.

questions.text: How did Luqman describe the importance of patience in his advice?
questions.answer: He said that patience in the face of trials is among the matters of 'firm determination' or great importance.

questions.text: What was Luqman's advice regarding arrogance and social behavior?
questions.answer: Do not turn your cheek away from people in contempt and do not walk insolently upon the earth.

questions.text: What did Luqman say about Allah's view of the arrogant and boastful?
questions.answer: Indeed, Allah does not like everyone who is self-deluded and boastful.

questions.text: What advice did Luqman give regarding the manner of walking and speaking?
questions.answer: Be moderate in your pace and lower your voice.

questions.text: Which animal's voice is used as a negative example for loud speech?
questions.answer: The donkey; its voice is described as the most disagreeable of sounds.

questions.text: How has Allah made the universe subservient to humans?
questions.answer: He has made subservient to us whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth and showered us with His visible and invisible favors.

questions.text: What is the excuse of those who argue about Allah without knowledge?
questions.answer: They say, 'We follow that which we found our fathers upon,' even if Shaitan is inviting them to the punishment of the Blaze.

questions.text: What does it mean to 'submit one's face to Allah' while doing good?
questions.answer: It means total sincere submission and excellence in action; such a person has grasped the firmest handhold (Al-Urwatul Wuthqa).

questions.text: What is the 'firmest handhold' (Al-Urwatul Wuthqa)?
questions.answer: A metaphor for the strongest, unbreakable bond of faith and success with Allah.

questions.text: Should the Prophet be grieved by the disbelief of the people?
questions.answer: No, for to Allah is their return, and He will inform them of what they did; He knows what is within the breasts.

questions.text: How long will the disbelievers be allowed to enjoy this life?
questions.answer: Allah gives them enjoyment for a little while, then He will drive them to a massive punishment.

questions.text: What do the polytheists admit when asked about the creator of the heavens and earth?
questions.answer: They admit and say, 'Allah,' yet they still fail to worship Him alone.

questions.text: Describe the parable of the trees and the seas regarding Allah's words.
questions.answer: If all trees were pens and the sea with seven more seas were ink, they would all be exhausted before the words of Allah are finished.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the difficulty of creating and resurrecting all of mankind?
questions.answer: It is but as the creation and resurrection of a single soul; He is the Hearing, the Seeing.

questions.text: What is the sign of Allah's power in the movement of the sun and moon?
questions.answer: He causes the night to enter the day and the day to enter the night, and subjected the sun and moon to run for a specified term.

questions.text: What is the 'Truth' (Al-Haqq) mentioned in the context of Allah's sovereignty?
questions.answer: That Allah is the only Reality, and whatever they call upon besides Him is falsehood.

questions.text: What is the sign of Allah's mercy regarding ships in the sea?
questions.answer: The ships sail through the sea by the favor of Allah so that He may show you His signs.

questions.text: Who are the ones who truly benefit from the signs of ships at sea?
questions.answer: Everyone who is constantly patient and deeply grateful.

questions.text: How do people react when a wave like a canopy covers them at sea?
questions.answer: They call upon Allah, being sincere to Him in religion; but when He brings them safely to land, some of them falter.

questions.text: What warning is given to mankind regarding the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Fear your Lord and fear a Day when no father will avail his son, nor will a son avail his father at all.

questions.text: What should people not be deceived by in this life?
questions.answer: They should not be deceived by the worldly life, nor should the Deceiver (Shaitan) deceive them about Allah.

questions.text: What are the 'Five Keys of the Unseen' mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: Knowledge of the Hour, the falling of rain, what is in the wombs, what a soul will earn tomorrow, and in what land a soul will die.

questions.text: Does anyone besides Allah know what they will earn tomorrow or where they will die?
questions.answer: No soul knows what it will earn tomorrow, and no soul knows in what land it will die.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the word 'Hikmah' in the context of Luqman?
questions.answer: Wisdom, which includes correct understanding, sound judgment, and acting upon the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'visible and invisible favor' (Ni'mah)?
questions.answer: Visible favors are physical blessings like health and wealth; invisible favors are spiritual ones like faith and guidance.

questions.text: Why is Shirk called a 'Great Injustice' (Zulmun Azim)?
questions.answer: Because it is giving the right of the Creator to the created, which is the most misplaced and unfair act possible.

questions.text: What is the 'Praise' (Al-Hamd) that Allah claims for Himself in this Surah?
questions.answer: The recognition that all gratitude and worship belong to Him as the Master of the heavens and earth.

questions.text: What is the 'Specified Term' (Ajal Musamma) for the sun and moon?
questions.answer: The fixed time until which they will continue to function before the Day of Resurrection.

questions.text: What is the 'Blaze' (Sa'ir)?
questions.answer: A name for the intense fire of Hell prepared for the disbelievers.

questions.text: What is the 'Deceiver' (Al-Gharur)?
questions.answer: Shaitan, who tricks people into prioritizing the world over the Hereafter.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His own nature at the very end of the Surah?
questions.answer: As Indeed, Allah is Knowing and Acquainted (Alimun Khabir).

questions.text: What is the lesson from the 'waves' at sea?
questions.answer: That humans often only recognize their total dependence on Allah when they are in extreme danger.

questions.text: What is the 'True Promise' (Wa'dullahi Haqq)?
questions.answer: The certainty of the resurrection and the accounting for all deeds.

questions.text: What is the significance of the mention of 'ink' and 'seas'?
questions.answer: To illustrate the infinite nature of Allah's knowledge and wisdom compared to human understanding.

questions.text: What is the 'Path of the Prophets' mentioned implicitly?
questions.answer: The way of wisdom, patience, and absolute monotheism taught by Luqman and the messengers.

questions.text: What is the 'Insolent' walk?
questions.answer: Walking with pride, vanity, and a sense of superiority over others.

questions.text: Why does Luqman advise his son to lower his voice?
questions.answer: To maintain modesty, respect, and avoid being compared to the disagreeable sound of a donkey.

questions.text: What is the 'Weakness upon weakness'?
questions.answer: The physical and emotional toll of pregnancy on a mother.

questions.text: What is the 'Return to Allah' mentioned throughout the Surah?
questions.answer: The inevitable meeting with the Creator for judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'Correct religion' mentioned in other Surahs as the 'firmest handhold' here?
questions.answer: Islam, the path of complete submission to the One God.

questions.text: What is the 'Visible sign' in the ships?
questions.answer: The buoyancy and movement of vessels over water as a gift for human travel and trade.

questions.text: What is the 'Knowledge of the Wombs'?
questions.answer: Allah's perfect knowledge of the gender, destiny, and character of every unborn child.

questions.text: What is the final summary of Luqman's character?
questions.answer: A man of profound wisdom who recognized that the essence of wisdom is gratitude and worship of Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Great injustice' (Shirk)?
questions.answer: Associating partners with Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Words of Allah' metaphor?
questions.answer: The infinite knowledge and decrees of the Creator.

questions.text: What is the 'Mustard seed' metaphor?
questions.answer: Smallest possible thing being within Allah's knowledge.

questions.text: What is the 'Voice of donkeys' metaphor?
questions.answer: Example of loud and annoying speech.

questions.text: What is the 'Wait of parents'?
questions.answer: Hardship and patience in raising children.

questions.text: What is the 'Sanctuary' mentioned implicitly?
questions.answer: The protection of faith in a world of trials.

questions.text: What is the 'Day of Judgment' description?
questions.answer: A day where no kinship or worldly bond can save anyone.

questions.text: What is the 'Acquainted' (Khabir) attribute?
questions.answer: Allah knowing the finest and deepest details of all things.

questions.text: What is the 'Guidance and Mercy'?
questions.answer: The role of the Quran for those who are Muhsinin.

questions.text: What is the 'Zakat'?
questions.answer: Obligatory charity for the poor.

questions.text: What is the 'Certainty' (Yaqin)?
questions.answer: Unshakeable belief in the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'Submission' (Islam)?
questions.answer: Total surrender to Allah's will.

questions.text: What is the 'Patience' (Sabr)?
questions.answer: Endurance and steadfastness in faith.

questions.text: What is the 'Enjoining what is right' (Amr bil Ma'ruf)?
questions.answer: Encouraging others to do good.

questions.text: What is the 'Forbidding what is wrong' (Nahi anil Munkar)?
questions.answer: Discouraging others from doing evil.

questions.text: What is the 'Moderation' in walking?
questions.answer: Balanced and humble movement without arrogance.

questions.text: What is the 'Lowering of the voice'?
questions.answer: Speaking calmly and respectfully.

questions.text: What is the 'Gratefulness' (Shukr)?
questions.answer: Recognizing and thanking Allah for His blessings.

questions.text: What is the 'Successful' outcome (Muflihun)?
questions.answer: Achieving Allah's pleasure and Paradise.
